events.event_description: Daniel Smith McCauley presented credentials as Agent and Consul General, establishing a liaison-level U.S. presence in Egypt. Though commissioned only as Consul General, he used the title Agent and Consul General with the acquiescence of the Department of State.

evidence.quote: Daniel Smith Mccauley, Agent/Consul General. Appointed: 1848-08-14. Credentials presented: 1849-03-17. Died at post on 1852-10-24. Note: Commissioned as Consul General only and used the title Agent and Consul General with the acquiescence of the Department of State.

events.event_description: William Sydney Thayer presented credentials as Consul General only (not Agent and Consul General), reducing the mission to a purely consular level in Cairo, the capital.

evidence.quote: William Sydney Thayer, Consul General. Appointed: 1861-03-20. Credentials presented: 1861-07-01. Died at post on 1864-04-10. Note: Commissioned as Consul General only and used only his consular title.

events.event_description: Charles Hale presented credentials as Agent and Consul General, restoring the liaison-level presence. Though commissioned only as Consul General, his title was changed to Agent and Consul General by Act of Congress.

evidence.quote: Charles Hale, Agent/Consul General. Appointed: 1864-05-18. Credentials presented: 1864-10-15. Presented recall on 1870-05-23. Note: Commissioned as Consul General only; not recommissioned when his title was changed to Agent and Consul General by Act of Congress approved on June 20, 1864.

events.event_description: Joseph Morton Howell was promoted to Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary and presented credentials, elevating the U.S. mission in Egypt from a liaison/agency to a full legation.

evidence.quote: Joseph Morton Howell, Agent/Consul General. Appointed: 1921-10-07. Promoted to Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary 1922-08-28. Joseph Morton Howell, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1922-06-21. Credentials presented: 1922-08-28.

events.event_description: Somerville Pinkney Tuck was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials, elevating the U.S. mission in Egypt from a legation to a full embassy.

evidence.quote: Somerville Pinkney Tuck, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1944-05-04. Credentials presented: 1944-06-14. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1946-10-10. Somerville Pinkney Tuck, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1946-09-19. Credentials presented: 1946-10-10.

events.event_description: The United Arab Republic broke diplomatic relations with the United States, ending formal U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: David Gulick Nes, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. The United Arab Republic broke relations with the United States 1967-06-06.

events.event_description: The United States established an Interests Section in the Spanish Embassy in Cairo, maintaining a limited diplomatic presence after the break in relations.

evidence.quote: On June 7, 1967, the United States established an Interests Section in the Spanish Embassy.

events.event_description: The U.S. Embassy in Cairo was reestablished, with Ambassador-designate Eilts in charge pending his formal commissioning and presentation of credentials.

evidence.quote: The Embassy in Cairo was reestablished on February 28, 1974, with Ambassador-designate Eilts in charge pending his nomination, commissioning, and presentation of his letter of credence.
